title: What Is Asset Allocation? Definition, Strategies & Portfolio Guide
metaDescription: What is asset allocation? Learn the definition, key strategies, and how to build a diversified portfolio. Complete guide with examples for every investor level.
excerpt: Asset allocation is the strategy of dividing your investment portfolio across different asset classes. Learn how to build a portfolio that balances risk and return.

title: What Is a Bear Market? Definition, Causes & How to Protect Your Portfolio
metaDescription: Understand what a bear market is, what causes it, historical examples, and strategies to protect and grow your portfolio during downturns.
excerpt: A bear market is a sustained decline of 20% or more in asset prices from recent highs, typically accompanied by widespread pessimism, economic contraction, and reduced investor confidence.

title: What Is a Bull Market? Definition, Phases & Investment Strategies
metaDescription: Learn what a bull market is, its phases, causes, and how investors can capitalize on sustained upward market trends.
excerpt: A bull market is a prolonged period of rising asset prices, typically defined as a 20% or greater increase from recent lows, driven by optimism and strong economic fundamentals.

title: What Is Dividend Yield? Definition, Formula & How to Calculate It
metaDescription: Learn what dividend yield is, how to calculate it, what constitutes a good yield, and how to use it in your investment strategy for income generation.
excerpt: Dividend yield is a financial ratio that shows how much a company pays out in dividends each year relative to its stock price, expressed as a percentage, helping investors assess income potential.

title: What Is Dollar-Cost Averaging? Definition, Strategy & Complete Guide
metaDescription: Learn what dollar-cost averaging (DCA) is, how it works, the formula behind it, real-world examples, and why it's one of the most powerful strategies for long-term investors.
excerpt: Dollar-cost averaging is an investment strategy where you invest a fixed amount of money at regular intervals regardless of price, reducing the impact of volatility and removing emotion from investing decisions.

title: What Is an ETF? Definition, Types & How They Work
metaDescription: Learn what ETFs are, the different types available, how they work, and why they have become one of the most popular investment vehicles.
excerpt: An ETF (Exchange-Traded Fund) is an investment fund that holds a collection of assets—like stocks, bonds, or commodities—and trades on a stock exchange throughout the day like a single share.

title: What Is Market Capitalization? Definition, Categories & Why It Matters
metaDescription: Understand market capitalization, how it is calculated, the different size categories, and why it matters for investment strategy and risk assessment.
excerpt: Market capitalization is the total market value of a company's outstanding shares, calculated by multiplying the current share price by the total number of shares, serving as a key measure of company size.

title: What Is Price-to-Earnings Ratio? Definition, Formula & How to Use It
metaDescription: What is P/E ratio? Learn the definition, formula, and how to use the price-to-earnings ratio for stock valuation. Complete guide with real examples.
excerpt: The P/E ratio measures a stock's price relative to its earnings. Learn how to calculate, interpret, and use P/E ratios for smarter investment decisions.
